definition: The system of verified badges on WhatsApp Business that signal authenticity to customers. As of 2025-2026, the green tick has been retired and replaced by a unified blue tick across all Meta platforms. There are two paths to getting verified: the free Official Business Account (OBA) route available only to API users who meet strict notability criteria, and the paid Meta Verified subscription available to WhatsApp Business App users in select regions.
target_audience: Any business using WhatsApp for customer communication that wants to build trust and reduce impersonation risk. Particularly important for businesses in industries prone to scams (financial services, e-commerce, healthcare) and brands with high public visibility that need to differentiate from imposters. The two paths serve very different audiences: OBA targets established brands with significant public presence, while Meta Verified targets smaller businesses willing to pay for credibility.
key_features: Blue tick badge appears next to your business name in chat threads, contact info, and search results -- customers see it before they even open a conversation. Two distinct verification paths: (1) OBA (Official Business Account) -- free, API-only, requires Meta to deem your business 'notable' based on organic media coverage, strong web presence, verified social profiles, and active WhatsApp usage. Applied through Meta Business Manager. (2) Meta Verified -- paid subscription through the WhatsApp Business App, available in select countries (India, Brazil, Indonesia, Colombia, and expanding). Provides the same blue tick plus impersonation protection, priority customer support, a custom web page, and multi-device support. Verified businesses get higher trust signals, which can improve message open rates and reduce the likelihood of being blocked or reported by recipients.
limitations: OBA approval is not guaranteed -- Meta's notability criteria are subjective and many legitimate businesses get rejected. There is no appeals process publicly documented for OBA rejections. Meta Verified is only available in select countries as of early 2026, leaving businesses in unsupported regions with OBA as the only option. Meta Verified requires an ongoing monthly subscription -- if you stop paying, you lose the badge. The blue tick does not unlock any additional technical features (no extra messaging limits, no special API access) -- it is purely a trust signal. Small businesses without media coverage or public notability may find it nearly impossible to qualify for the free OBA path. Existing green tick holders were automatically converted to blue tick with no action needed.
pricing: OBA (API path): Completely free. No subscription, no one-time fee. But requires the WhatsApp Business API setup, which itself involves BSP costs and Meta's per-message fees. Meta Verified (App path): Pricing varies by region. In India, plans start at approximately INR 639/month (~$7.50/month) for the base tier (1 verified channel, 4 linked devices) up to INR 18,900/month (~$225/month) for premium tiers. In other markets, pricing starts around $11.99/month on web or $14.99/month on mobile for basic plans. The Business plan at INR 1,399/month (~$17/month) adds 3 verified channels and 6 linked devices. Hidden cost consideration: for OBA, you need the API infrastructure (BSP + Meta message fees), which far exceeds the Meta Verified subscription cost -- but you get the full API feature set along with it.
scalability_ceiling: The badge itself has no scalability limits -- once verified, it applies to all conversations regardless of volume. However, the paths have different implications: OBA verification through the API gives you access to the full tier system (up to 100K+ messages/day), while Meta Verified on the App still leaves you with the App's inherent limits (256 broadcast recipients, limited devices). For growing businesses, the OBA path is the better long-term play because it comes bundled with API scalability.
setup_complexity: OBA (API path): Complexity 4 out of 5. Requires: (1) Full WhatsApp Business API setup through a BSP, (2) Meta Business Verification (submit business registration documents, tax IDs), (3) Building a case for 'notability' -- professional website, verified social profiles, media coverage, (4) Submitting the OBA application through Meta Business Manager, (5) Waiting up to 3+ business days for review. No guarantee of approval. Meta Verified (App path): Complexity 1 out of 5. Open the WhatsApp Business App, navigate to Meta Verified settings, choose a plan, pay, and verification is granted almost immediately. Much simpler but limited to supported regions.
migration_complexity: If you have Meta Verified on the App and migrate to the API, your paid Meta Verified subscription does not transfer. You would need to apply for OBA separately, and there's no guarantee of approval. Conversely, if you have OBA on the API and (for some reason) move back to the App, the OBA badge is lost. The verification paths are tied to the platform (App vs API), not to the phone number. Green tick to blue tick conversion was automatic and required no action.
data_compliance: Verification itself does not change data handling or GDPR obligations. However, the OBA path requires Meta Business Verification, which involves submitting business documents (registration certificates, tax IDs) to Meta. These documents are processed under Meta's privacy policy. For GDPR-sensitive businesses, ensure you understand how Meta handles these verification documents. The Meta Verified subscription path collects payment information and subscription data. Neither path changes how message data is stored or processed.
verification_path: This is the core topic. Two paths: (1) OBA -- free, API-only, strict notability requirements, no guaranteed approval, 3+ business day review. Best for established brands with public presence. (2) Meta Verified -- paid subscription, App-only (currently), available in select countries, near-instant approval, ongoing cost. Best for smaller businesses in supported regions who want quick credibility. Both result in the same blue tick badge visible to customers. The practical difference is cost structure and eligibility requirements.
message_template_rules: Verification status does not change template rules. API users still need pre-approved templates for outbound messages regardless of whether they have OBA. However, having OBA (verified status) may indirectly help template approval rates, as Meta views verified businesses more favorably. App users with Meta Verified do not use templates -- they continue sending freeform messages within the App's normal capabilities.
messaging_limits: OBA verification can positively influence tier progression on the API. Meta Business Verification (a prerequisite for OBA) is required to move beyond Tier 0 (250/day). Having OBA doesn't directly increase your tier, but the verification steps needed to get OBA (business verification, high quality rating) are the same steps that enable tier upgrades. Meta Verified on the App does not change the App's inherent messaging limits.
ai_automation_policy: Verification status does not change AI automation rules. The January 2026 ban on general-purpose AI chatbots applies to all API users regardless of OBA status. Verified businesses must follow the same structured-automation-only policy as everyone else.
quality_risk: Verified businesses (OBA) are held to the same quality standards as unverified ones. A poor quality rating (Red) can still restrict your messaging even with the blue tick. In fact, losing quality while verified may attract more scrutiny from Meta. There is no public documentation on whether OBA status can be revoked due to sustained poor quality, but it is a reasonable assumption that serious violations could trigger a review.
ads_integration: The blue tick appears in CTWA (Click-to-WhatsApp Ads) conversations, which can significantly improve ad conversion rates. When a user clicks an ad and sees a verified badge in the chat, trust is established immediately. This is particularly valuable for cold audiences who have never interacted with your brand. Both OBA (API) and Meta Verified (App) badges appear in CTWA-initiated chats.
ecommerce_features: Verification does not unlock additional e-commerce features, but the trust signal matters enormously for online purchases. Customers are more likely to complete in-chat purchases, share payment details, or click product catalog links when they see a verified badge. For WooCommerce stores using WhatsApp for order support or marketing, the blue tick can measurably reduce cart abandonment in chat-based sales flows.
wpchat_relevance: WPChat.com can display your WhatsApp verification status context on your WordPress site -- showing visitors that your WhatsApp channel is officially verified before they even start a conversation. This pre-chat trust signal complements the in-chat blue tick. For businesses that have invested in getting verified (either path), WPChat ensures that trust carries through from the website to the chat experience. WPChat's multi-agent profiles can also reflect verified business identity, creating a consistent brand experience.
best_for: OBA: Established brands with significant media coverage, strong web presence, and active WhatsApp usage who want the credibility boost without a monthly fee. Meta Verified: Small-to-medium businesses in supported regions (India, Brazil, Indonesia, Colombia) who want quick verification without the technical overhead of the API. Both: Any business where trust is a conversion factor -- financial services, healthcare, e-commerce, professional services.
not_suited_for: OBA: New or obscure businesses with no media coverage or public presence -- the application will likely be rejected. Meta Verified: Businesses outside supported regions, or those who object to paying a monthly fee for what is essentially a trust badge. Both: Businesses that primarily serve returning customers who already trust them -- the badge adds less value when your audience already knows you.
cta_angle: Getting verified is only half the battle -- customers need to see that badge before they can trust it. WPChat.com puts a professional WhatsApp chat widget front and center on your WordPress site, so visitors discover your verified WhatsApp channel the moment they land on your page, not just when they happen to have your number saved.
uncertain: Meta Verified exact pricing outside India
